what_it_does: Records an appended evidence note for an open issue, asserting that a byte-identical message replay originated at the ChatGPT connector/client send side rather than the model or channel store, with timestamps and checksums.
observations: Issue state is open and links to item-60edac60 (same_law) and item-04e0b77b (sourced_from)
Evidence draws on remote_mcp_audit.jsonl (connector audit) and the cc_channels store (bodies+checksums)
The 8-question message has body-checksum c1d75f and appears at 08:47:33 and 12:15:27 byte-identical
The 12:15:27 occurrence is a real audited connector send-call {op:send, to:ch-3mpkjg3r} with subject operator (ebe5f9c7)
Conclusion: the ChatGPT/OpenAI-side connector re-issued a prior tool-call, so the replay is at the send/client side
No fix is proposed; the entry is explicitly evidence-only
standouts: Byte-identical checksum c1d75f at two timestamps 3h28m apart
12:15:27 occurrence is a real audited connector send-call, not a channel-store duplicate
Conclusion locates the origin at the send/client side and supersedes the channel-reinjection hypothesis
conventions: Follows ledger YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses markdown bullet list for evidence
References related board items via structured link kinds
Explicitly marks the note as evidence-only with no fix proposed
concerns: No proposed fix or mitigation despite a decisive-sounding conclusion
Relies on external audit files and stores whose contents are not present in this file
Byte-identical conclusion assumes no checksum collision and identical serialization
notes: This is an evidence-only issue append that pins a message replay on the ChatGPT connector/client send side using checksum and audit timestamps.
questions: What is the exact schema of remote_mcp_audit.jsonl and the cc_channels store?
How was checksum c1d75f computed over the message body?
What triggered the connector to re-issue the prior tool-call at 12:15:27?
What does the linked same_law item-60edac60 state?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to append decisive evidence about replay origin; the file does exactly that, so they match.
apparent_themes: Distinguishing model behavior from connector/client behavior
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a precise causal origin for a duplicate message by correlating channel-store checksums with connector audit logs, so the system can later target the correct component for a fix.
novelty: Uses byte-identical checksum across a 3.5-hour gap to argue that a reasoning model could not have re-composed the same message, shifting blame to the connector client.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, checksum-correlated, audit-backed hypothesis that a message replay originated as a re-issued connector send-call, which a fused system would keep as a forensic anchor for debugging duplicate delivery.
summary_for_embedding: An open ledger issue item that appends evidence claiming a byte-identical message replay originated at the ChatGPT connector/client send side, citing checksum c1d75f at 08:47:33 and 12:15:27 and a real audited send-call.
intention_for_embedding: To record a forensically supported conclusion about which side of the system re-issued a prior tool-call, without yet proposing a fix.
